headline: Canal+ 'blacklist' threat fuels tensions in the French film industry
description: The number of signatories to the anti-Bolloré open letter continues to grow as the CNC, France's national film board, tries to ease tensions and many producers, actors and directors defended Canal+'s work.

Ebola outbreak in the DRC: West's aid cuts 'contributed to the weakening of the surveillance chain'
description: In an interview, the head of Africa CDC, the African Union's health agency, denounces the impact that drastic reductions in international aid have had on the delayed management of the ongoing outbreak.

headline: Michelin guide transforms itself to become global benchmark for the art of living
description: In response to competition from digital platforms, the restaurant guide has begun a major shift, expanding both its scope of expertise and its geographic influence.

headline: Cannes 2026: Pedro Almodovar's 'Bitter Christmas' explores writing and self-reflection
description: In his seventh film competing for the Palme d'Or, the Spanish filmmaker examines his own creative process through the character of a writer in crisis, played by Leonardo Sbaraglia.

headline: How Le Pen's party has shifted its economic platform over 15 years
description: Ever since Marine Le Pen took control of the far-right Rassemblement National in 2011, it has struggled to present a clear economic platform, to the point of muddying its political identity and sparking questions about its plans.

Gaza's vanished past lives on through photographs accidentally rediscovered in Marseille
description: Kegham Djeghalian, a photographer and Armenian genocide survivor, settled in Gaza in 1944. He documented the Palestinian territory through portraits and reports until he died in 1981. His grandson has chosen to exhibit his images of ordinary and joyful moments in Gaza, now devastated by two years of war.

headline: Deaths in the Mediterranean: An ongoing tragedy met with widespread indifference
description: 'Migrants in the Mediterranean: The mechanics of silence' (1/4). The first quarter of 2026 was one of the deadliest since 2014 for migrants departing from Tunisia and Libya toward Europe, with 765 deaths recorded by the International Organization for Migration. The EU system in the region is currently aimed more at containing the movement of people than saving lives.

headline: Trump's ballroom obsession worries Republicans
description: The costly and monumental real estate project has repeatedly been mentioned in presidential speeches, highlighting the billionaire's disconnect from public concerns. Only 28% of citizens deemed the construction necessary.
